Autores: A. D. Athapattu; P. S. B. Dassanayake; G. S. C. Nanayakkara; S. N. Liyanage; G. A. I. Devindi; R. Ragel; T. Dissanayake; I. Nawinne
Titulo: Self-Paced Brain-Computer Interface on Sensorimotor Rhythms for Controlling Virtual Objects
Anotation: Artigo meio fora de escopo já que fala "s. In this research, we explore the possibility of using the techniques used in the motor-imagery method for non-motor imagery mental tasks. The intention here is to use them in virtual object-controlling applications. The research was done with five different classification models using features from the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) and Wavelet Transform (WT). The K-nearest neighbor model with features obtained with FFT continuously sustained its performance with a high 55% true positive rate.", mas é bom ler para ter certeza.

Autores: Z. Wang; C. Cao; Y. Zhou; L. Chen; B. Gu; S. Liu; M. Xu; F. He; D. Ming
Titulo: Integrating EEG and NIRS improves BCI performance during motor imagery
Anotation: Esse artigo é um pouco diferente. Fala de integrar EEG com fNIRS, sem contar o fato do dataset utilizado ser bem especifico. Sem contar que o objetivo é a construção de um classificador. Não fala nada de filtro temporal se foi usado, quando e porque. Ler ele buscando o modelo e se tiver filtro temporal da pra colocar na SLR se não tá fora. Artigo fala "This study aims to improve the BCI performance by integrating electroencephalographic and cerebral hemodynamic patterns. Sixteen volunteers participated in a seven-session motor imagery (MI) based visual-haptic neurofeedback training (NFT) experiment. Electroencephalogram (EEG) and near infrared spectroscopy (NIRS) signals were synchronously recorded during the transient NFT. Time-frequency and topology analysis demonstrated that repetitive and continuous visual-haptic NFT conditions could induce an enhancement on cortical activations. Additionally, a classifier calibration strategy was proposed by integrating both the EEG and NIRS information into a strong classifier."

Autores: R. Govindarajan; M. Haritha; M. O. Sabri; B. Priyanka; T. Mrudula; R. V
Titulo: Optimal Deep Learning Model and Non-linear Features Extraction with Classification for Innovative Brain–Computer Interaction Using EEG Signals
Anotation: O artigo fala "The Optimal Deep Learning Model and Nonlinear Features Extraction with Classification for Innovative Brain–Computer Interaction Using EEG Signals (ODECBC) model enhances BCI systems by using advanced signal processing and hybrid classification methods, improving accuracy and reliability in detecting mental states like Idle, Mental Arithmetic, and Motor Imagery. It utilizes data from 18 healthy individuals, capturing high-resolution EEG signals at 2048 Hz and fNIRS signals at 13.3 Hz to ensure consistent data collection. To enhance signal quality through wavelet denoising and a Butterworth filter to reduce noise and interference, down-sampling signals are down-sampled to 200 Hz for easier processing. Nonlinear feature extraction techniques help reveal brain signal patterns, aiding in understanding cognitive functions.". Tem filtro temporal explicito, mas parece ser dependente do fNIRS e avalia não só MI
